intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but lacks the anchor rabbit in the current frame.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant material visible in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage present.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos foliage visible, but the scale anchor (white rabbit) is missing from the current frame.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage present.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Appears to contain only soil/substrate; no active growth.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Pothos specimen shows minimal leaf structure; health is poor due to lack of visible growth.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Substrate appears consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of a metallic spoon in the yellow pot (T-3) and a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or marker) in the yellow pot (T-1, T-4, Current).

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_4: Introduction of a black marker/sensor in the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T_4_to_T_3: Introduction of a metal spoon into the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T_3_to_T_2: Removal of the metal spoon; continued absence of plant growth.
temporal_deltas.T_2_to_T_1: Re-introduction of the white cylindrical marker.
temporal_deltas.T_1_to_Current: The white rabbit anchor has been removed from the P3 pot area.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe neglect or dormancy. The lack of foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests systemic failure. The Pothos in P3 is the only surviving specimen, though it shows no signs of active development.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a highly unstable environment. The registry provided does not match the current physical reality, which shows a significant loss of plant life. The pots are largely devoid of vegetation, with only a single Pothos specimen remaining. The frequent introduction and removal of non-botanical items (spoon, sensor) suggests an active, perhaps experimental, human intervention that is not conducive to the health of the specimens.
